title: High-Stakes AI Execution Controls
summary: Explains why the real challenge in serious AI systems is controlled execution, verification, auditability, and failure recovery rather than intelligence alone.

verification.notes: Derived from the user's notes about execution controls, verification, auditability, and failure recovery.

claims: In serious operating environments, the hardest AI problem is controlled action rather than intelligence alone.
Security, policy enforcement, verification, auditability, and failure recovery are central to business trust in agent systems.
Controlled execution is what turns AI from a novelty layer into an operational system leaders can trust and scale.
proof_points: The notes explicitly state that the hardest problem is safely turning intelligence into action.
The notes group verification, auditability, deterministic execution, reproducibility, observability, and failure recovery ahead of raw performance.
The notes identify execution controls and real-time state consistency as central architecture concerns in high-stakes systems.
